[Subagent Task]:

Continue building Neon Trivia VR (round 5). The dev server may need restarting â€” if you need it, run `cd ~/workspace/neon-trivia && npx vite --host &` and wait a few seconds. The game is at ~/workspace/neon-trivia/.

CURRENT STATE:
- 2,444 lines, 17 uikitml files (18 panels per status), 400 questions, 9 game modes, 40 achievements, 6 themes
- Build time: 240/360 minutes
- Has: power-ups, review panel, challenge mode (vs AI), streak effects, category stats, difficulty stats, game history, haptics, trail particles, animated ring + orbiting lights
- Zero type errors, deployed to gh-pages

YOUR TASK â€” Round 5. Focus on polish, additional content, and new visual features.

### 1. Add 100 More Questions (reach 500 total)
Add 10 more questions per category (categories 0-9) to the QUESTIONS array using the `qb()` helper. Write fresh, interesting, accurate trivia. Avoid duplicating existing topics. Mix of easy/medium/hard difficulties.

### 2. NEW: Endless Mode ("endless")
Add a 10th game mode â€” `endless`. Rules:
- Questions never end â€” recycle through the pool shuffled fresh each cycle
- No timer per question (like practice mode)
- Difficulty dynamically increases: start with easy questions, after 10 correct switch to medium, after 25 correct switch to hard
- 3 lives â€” wrong answer costs a life; game over at 0 lives
- Add `lives: number` to GameState (default 3), `dynamicDifficulty: Difficulty` (default 'easy')
- Add MODE_QUESTION_COUNT entry: `endless: 99999`
- Add `btn-endless` to `ui/modeselect.uikitml`: "ENDLESS (3 Lives)"
- Wire in mode select handler â€” goes directly to game (skip difficulty select since it's dynamic)
- In `selectAnswer()`: if mode is 'endless' and wrong answer, decrement lives instead of just breaking streak. If lives === 0, endGame.
- In `showQuestion()`: when `gs.currentIndex >= gs.questions.length`, reshuffle and reset index to loop
- In update HUD: show lives remaining "Lives: âť¤âť¤âť¤" â€” use ASCII hearts or just "Lives: 3"
- In gameover: show "Survived X questions!" for endless mode

### 3. Improved Visual Polish â€” Holographic Panel Effects
Add a subtle floating animation to all visible panels:
- In the `update()` method, for each visible panel (offset Y > -50), apply a gentle sine-wave vertical oscillation: `offset[1] += Math.sin(time * 0.8 + panelIndex * 0.5) * 0.001` 
- This creates a slight "floating in space" holographic feel
- Keep the amplitude very small (0.001) so it's atmospheric, not distracting
- Skip panels during active gameplay (don't animate question/answer/hud panels while `gameRunning` is true â€” only animate menu panels)

### 4. Achievement Notifications Panel
When achievements unlock during endGame, show a special notification:
- Create `ui/achvnotify.uikitml` â€” a smaller panel (width 300, height 120) with golden styling:
  - `lbl-achv-title`: "ACHIEVEMENT UNLOCKED!" in gold
  - `lbl-achv-name`: the achievement name
  - `lbl-achv-desc`: the achievement description
- Add to PANEL_CONFIGS: `achvnotify: { config: './ui/achvnotify.json', y: -0.25, z: -1.0 }`
- Add to Screen (no â€” this is an overlay that appears alongside gameover)
- Instead of the simple toast for achievements, show this panel briefly (3 seconds) positioned below the gameover panel
- Add a module-level variable `achvNotifyTimer` and `pendingAchievements: number[]` (indices of newly unlocked achievements)
- In update(): tick achvNotifyTimer, cycle through pending achievements, hide panel when done
- Wire the qualify event in TriviaSystem

### 5. Improved Leaderboard
Expand the leaderboard system:
- Increase from 10 to 15 visible rows in `ui/leaderboard.uikitml` (add row10-row14)
- Make the panel taller (height 520)
- Add mode filter buttons: `btn-all`, `btn-classic-filter`, `btn-challenge-filter` that filter leaderboard entries by mode
- Add `leaderboardFilter: string` module variable (default 'all')
- `updateLeaderboardUI()` filters entries based on the current filter before displaying

### 6. README Overhaul
Rewrite `README.md` at `~/workspace/neon-trivia/README.md` with:
- Game title and description
- Link to live demo: https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-trivia/
- Features list organized by category
- How to play (controls for browser and XR)
- Game modes descriptions (all 10)
- Tech stack: IWSDK 0.4.x, TypeScript, PanelUI/UIKitML
- Build/run instructions
- Credits

### 7. Performance Optimization
- In the particle update loop, skip particles that are below Y=-10 (off-screen) â€” don't waste cycles updating their X position
- In the wireframe animation, only animate wireframes that are within 15 units of the camera â€” skip distant ones
- Cache frequently accessed DOM elements in local variables rather than re-querying in hot loops

CRITICAL RULES:
- All imports from `@iwsdk/core`, never from `three`
- No `(world as any)` â€” world is fully typed
- No HTML DOM UI â€” all PanelUI uikitml
- Use typed input: `world.input.keyboard`, `world.input.xr.gamepads`
- Per-frame logic in ECS System `update()` only
- Panel readiness via `subscribe('qualify', ...)` â€” no setTimeout
- uikitml: quoted numeric attributes, ASCII only in text content
- Panel config paths relative: `'./ui/name.json'`

After ALL changes:
1. `npx tsc --noEmit` â€” MUST pass with 0 errors
2. `npm run build` â€” must succeed
3. Git commit and deploy:
```bash
cd ~/workspace/neon-trivia
git add -A && git commit -m "Round 5: 500 questions, endless mode, achievement notifications, leaderboard filters, visual polish"
git push origin main
npm run build
cd dist && rm -rf .git && git init && git checkout -b gh-pages && git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy round 5"
git remote add origin https://github.com/ellyz2426/neon-trivia.git
git push -f origin gh-pages
cd .. && rm -rf dist/.git
```

4. Update `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json`:
   - Add round 5 to rounds array
   - Update total_build_minutes (target: get to ~300+)
   - Update features_completed, lines_of_code, uikitml_panels
   - Set status to "waiting_continuation" if under 360, "complete" if >= 360
   - Set last_updated_utc

5. Append to BUILD_LOG.md, overwrite latest-build-summary.md

## IWSDK Build Check-in â€” Continuation Protocol

You are an hourly sentinel job. Your job is to check whether the overnight IWSDK build needs to continue.

### Decision logic:

1. **Read** `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json`

2. **If `status` is `"building"`** â†’ Check for staleness first:
   - Read `build_start_utc` and `rounds` from the status file
   - If `rounds` is empty AND more than 30 minutes have elapsed since `build_start_utc`, the master job stalled mid-scaffold. **Treat this as `"waiting_continuation"`** â€” fall through to step 5 and take over the build yourself.
   - Otherwise, a build agent is genuinely running. Do nothing. Report: "Build still in progress."

3. **If `status` is `"complete"` or total_build_minutes >= 360** â†’ The 6-hour target has been met. Do nothing. Report: "Build complete, target reached."

4. **If `status` is `"idle"` and no `date` set for today** â†’ The midnight job hasn't run yet or failed. Do nothing. Report: "No build started today."

5. **If `status` is `"waiting_continuation"` and total_build_minutes < 360** â†’ The previous round stopped early. You must continue building. Follow the continuation protocol below.

### âš ď¸Ź CRITICAL: Spatial UI Only â€” NO HTML Overlays

**Before expanding features, audit existing code for HTML DOM UI anti-patterns.**

Search the codebase for: `document.createElement`, `innerHTML`, `<div>`, `<canvas>`, `.style.`, `document.body`, `getElementById` on `document` (not on `UIKitDocument`). If any game UI uses HTML DOM overlays, **fix them first** by converting to `.uikitml` PanelUI panels before adding new features.

All game UI must use IWSDK's PanelUI system:
- `.uikitml` templates compiled by `@iwsdk/vite-plugin-uikitml`
- `PanelUI` component for 3D panels
- `Follower` component for head-locked HUDs
- `ScreenSpace` component for browser overlays that auto-adapt in XR
- Read section **4b** of the IWSDK dev skill at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`

### âš ď¸Ź CRITICAL: XR Verification Gate

**You MUST verify all UI and interactions work in XR BEFORE expanding features.**

Use IWSDK CLI tools (`npx iwsdk xr`, `npx iwsdk ecs pause/step/snapshot`) to confirm:
- All PanelUI panels render and are readable in XR
- XR controller laser pointer can interact with all buttons/menus
- HUD panels follow the player's head correctly
- No HTML overlays remain in the codebase

If XR verification fails, fix the issues before adding anything else.

### Continuation protocol:

When continuing a build:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`

2. **Read the status file** to understand:
   - What game is being built (`game_name`, `game_dir`)
   - How many minutes have been accumulated (`total_build_minutes`)
   - What features are already done (`features_completed`)
   - How many rounds have run (`rounds`)

3. **Update status file**: Set `status` to `"building"`, set `last_updated_utc`

4. **Assess the current codebase**: Read source files, check for type errors, understand what exists

5. **Plan expansion**: Based on what's missing, plan the next set of improvements. Prioritize:
   - Fix any type errors or broken code first
   - **Convert any HTML DOM UI to PanelUI spatial panels** (this blocks everything else)
   - XR controller support (if not yet working end-to-end)
   - **Verify all UI and interactions in XR** before proceeding
   - Visual polish and new content
   - New game modes
   - Audio improvements
   - Performance optimization
   - README and documentation

6. **Build**: Implement the planned improvements. Push commits and redeploy to gh-pages at milestones.

7. **When done**: Update the status file:
   - Append new round to `rounds` array with timing and stats
   - Update `total_build_minutes`
   - Update `features_completed`
   - Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` if still under 6h, or `"complete"` if â‰Ą6h
   - Set `last_updated_utc`

8. **Capture journal**: Copy your session JSONL into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo. Commit and push.

9. **Update build log and summary**: Append to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` and overwrite `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md` with current state.

### GitHub deploy commands:
```
cd ~/workspace/<game-name>
git add -A && git commit -m "<description>" && git push origin main
npm run build
cd dist && rm -rf .git && git init && git checkout -b gh-pages && git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
git remote add origin https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git
git push -f origin gh-pages
cd .. && rm -rf dist/.git
```

Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope. Use direct gh-pages branch deployment only.

{"content":"# Neon Trivia VR

A neon-lit holographic trivia game built for WebXR and browser. Test your knowledge across 10 categories with 500 questions, 10 game modes, and a full achievement system â€” all rendered in immersive 3D spatial UI.

**[Play Now](https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-trivia/)**

---

## Features

### Trivia Content
- **500 trivia questions** across 10 categories
- **10 categories**: Science, History, Geography, Entertainment, Sports, Technology, Nature, Food & Drink, Arts & Culture, General Knowledge
- **3 difficulty levels**: Easy, Medium, Hard with per-difficulty accuracy tracking
- **Dynamic difficulty** in Endless mode â€” auto-scales from easy to hard

### Game Modes
1. **Classic** â€” 20 questions, standard rules
2. **Speed** â€” 60-second timer, answer as many as you can
3. **Streak** â€” One wrong answer ends the game
4. **Category** â€” Pick a category, 20 questions deep
5. **Daily Challenge** â€” Same questions globally each day (seeded PRNG)
6. **Blitz** â€” 10 seconds per question
7. **Marathon** â€” 50 questions, endurance test
8. **Practice** â€” No timer, no pressure
9. **Challenge** â€” Head-to-head vs AI opponent with difficulty-based probability
10. **Endless** â€” 3 lives, no timer, difficulty scales dynamically. Survive as long as you can!

### Progression & Rewards
- **XP & Leveling** system with combo multipliers
- **40 achievements** with persistent tracking and unlock notifications

## IWSDK Build Check-in â€” Continuation Protocol

You are an hourly sentinel job. Your job is to check whether the overnight IWSDK build needs to continue.

### Decision logic:

1. **Read** `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json`

2. **If `status` is `"building"`** â†’ Check for staleness first:
   - Read `build_start_utc` and `rounds` from the status file
   - If `rounds` is empty AND more than 30 minutes have elapsed since `build_start_utc`, the master job stalled mid-scaffold. **Treat this as `"waiting_continuation"`** â€” fall through to step 5 and take over the build yourself.
   - Otherwise, a build agent is genuinely running. Do nothing. Report: "Build still in progress."

3. **If `status` is `"complete"` or total_build_minutes >= 360** â†’ The 6-hour target has been met. Do nothing. Report: "Build complete, target reached."

4. **If `status` is `"idle"` and no `date` set for today** â†’ The midnight job hasn't run yet or failed. Do nothing. Report: "No build started today."

5. **If `status` is `"waiting_continuation"` and total_build_minutes < 360** â†’ The previous round stopped early. You must continue building. Follow the continuation protocol below.

### âš ď¸Ź CRITICAL: Spatial UI Only â€” NO HTML Overlays

**Before expanding features, audit existing code for HTML DOM UI anti-patterns.**

Search the codebase for: `document.createElement`, `innerHTML`, `<div>`, `<canvas>`, `.style.`, `document.body`, `getElementById` on `document` (not on `UIKitDocument`). If any game UI uses HTML DOM overlays, **fix them first** by converting to `.uikitml` PanelUI panels before adding new features.

All game UI must use IWSDK's PanelUI system:
- `.uikitml` templates compiled by `@iwsdk/vite-plugin-uikitml`
- `PanelUI` component for 3D panels
- `Follower` component for head-locked HUDs
- `ScreenSpace` component for browser overlays that auto-adapt in XR
- Read section **4b** of the IWSDK dev skill at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`

### âš ď¸Ź CRITICAL: XR Verification Gate

**You MUST verify all UI and interactions work in XR BEFORE expanding features.**

Use IWSDK CLI tools (`npx iwsdk xr`, `npx iwsdk ecs pause/step/snapshot`) to confirm:
- All PanelUI panels render and are readable in XR
- XR controller laser pointer can interact with all buttons/menus
- HUD panels follow the player's head correctly
- No HTML overlays remain in the codebase

If XR verification fails, fix the issues before adding anything else.

### Continuation protocol:

When continuing a build:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`

2. **Read the status file** to understand:
   - What game is being built (`game_name`, `game_dir`)
   - How many minutes have been accumulated (`total_build_minutes`)
   - What features are already done (`features_completed`)
   - How many rounds have run (`rounds`)

3. **Update status file**: Set `status` to `"building"`, set `last_updated_utc`

4. **Assess the current codebase**: Read source files, check for type errors, understand what exists

5. **Plan expansion**: Based on what's missing, plan the next set of improvements. Prioritize:
   - Fix any type errors or broken code first
   - **Convert any HTML DOM UI to PanelUI spatial panels** (this blocks everything else)
   - XR controller support (if not yet working end-to-end)
   - **Verify all UI and interactions in XR** before proceeding
   - Visual polish and new content
   - New game modes
   - Audio improvements
   - Performance optimization
   - README and documentation

6. **Build**: Implement the planned improvements. Push commits and redeploy to gh-pages at milestones.

7. **When done**: Update the status file:
   - Append new round to `rounds` array with timing and stats
   - Update `total_build_minutes`
   - Update `features_completed`
   - Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` if still under 6h, or `"complete"` if â‰Ą6h
   - Set `last_updated_utc`

8. **Capture journal**: Copy your session JSONL into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo. Commit and push.

9. **Update build log and summary**: Append to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` and overwrite `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md` with current state.

### GitHub deploy commands:
```
cd ~/workspace/<game-name>
git add -A && git commit -m "<description>" && git push origin main
npm run build
cd dist && rm -rf .git && git init && git checkout -b gh-pages && git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
git remote add origin https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git
git push -f origin gh-pages
cd .. && rm -rf dist/.git
```

Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope. Use direct gh-pages branch deployment only.

item.output:
# Neon Trivia VR

A neon-lit holographic trivia game built for WebXR and browser. Test your knowledge across 10 categories with 500 questions, 10 game modes, and a full achievement system â€” all rendered in immersive 3D spatial UI.

**[Play Now](https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-trivia/)**

---

## Features

### Trivia Content
- **500 trivia questions** across 10 categories
- **10 categories**: Science, History, Geography, Entertainment, Sports, Technology, Nature, Food & Drink, Arts & Culture, General Knowledge
- **3 difficulty levels**: Easy, Medium, Hard with per-difficulty accuracy tracking
- **Dynamic difficulty** in Endless mode â€” auto-scales from easy to hard

### Game Modes
1. **Classic** â€” 20 questions, standard rules
2. **Speed** â€” 60-second timer, answer as many as you can
3. **Streak** â€” One wrong answer ends the game
4. **Category** â€” Pick a category, 20 questions deep
5. **Daily Challenge** â€” Same questions globally each day (seeded PRNG)
6. **Blitz** â€” 10 seconds per question
7. **Marathon** â€” 50 questions, endurance test
8. **Practice** â€” No timer, no pressure
9. **Challenge** â€” Head-to-head vs AI opponent with difficulty-based answering probability
10. **Endless** â€” 3 lives, no timer, difficulty scales dynamically. Survive as long as you can!

### Progression & Rewards
- **XP & Leveling** system with combo multipliers
[75 more lines in file. Use offset=31 to continue.]

- **40 achievements** with persistent tracking and golden unlock notifications
- **Top-15 leaderboard** with mode filtering (All / Classic / Challenge)
- **Career stats** dashboard with per-category and per-difficulty accuracy
- **Game history** tracking (last 20 games)
- **Daily streak** tracking

### Gameplay Features
- **3 lifelines**: 50:50, Skip, Hint
- **2 power-ups**: Double Points (2x), Time Freeze
- **Combo scoring** with multipliers up to x10
- **Streak visual effects**: "ON FIRE" at 5+, "UNSTOPPABLE" at 10+ with golden particles
- **Post-game review** with scrollable question results and pagination
- **6 neon themes**: Holodeck, Crimson, Toxic, Ultraviolet, Solar, Arctic

### Visual & Audio
- **3D holodeck environment** with wireframe decorations, particle effects, fog, and neon grids
- **Animated torus ring** with dynamic rotation
- **4 orbiting point lights** with varying heights
- **250 atmospheric particles** with additive blending
- **Holographic panel float** â€” subtle sine-wave oscillation on menu panels
- **Achievement notification overlays** with golden styling
- **Trail particle effects** on combo streaks
- **XR controller haptic feedback** for correct/wrong/milestone events
- **HUD bounce** on correct answers, red flash on wrong

## How to Play

### Browser Controls
| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `1-4` | Select answer A-D |
| `F` | Use 50:50 lifeline |
| `S` | Skip question |
| `H` | Use hint |
| `D` | Activate Double Points |
| `T` | Activate Time Freeze |
| `Esc` / `P` | Pause game |

### VR Controls
| Input | Action |
|-------|--------|
| Laser pointer | Select UI buttons |
| `A` button | Activate Double Points |
| `B` button | Pause game |

## Tech Stack

- **IWSDK** 0.4.x â€” Immersive Web SDK (Meta's WebXR framework)
- **TypeScript** â€” Fully typed, zero `as any` casts
- **PanelUI / UIKitML** â€” All 19 spatial UI panels, zero HTML DOM overlays
- **Vite** â€” Build tooling with UIKitML plugin
- **localStorage** â€” Persistent stats, achievements, leaderboard, settings

## Build & Run

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Start dev server
npx vite --host

# Type check
npx tsc --noEmit

# Production build
npm run build
```

## Credits

Built with IWSDK (Immersive Web SDK) by Meta's WebXR team. Designed for dual runtime â€” works in both standard browsers and WebXR headsets.

IWSDK Daily Build #86 â€” 2026-07-03
